heading: Connect from Microsoft Excel
body:
Start Microsoft Excel and open a blank worksheet.

- Click the Data on the toolbar.

- On the toolbar, click New Database Query, then click
  From Database.

  If the New Database Query button is not displayed,
  click Get External Data, then
  New Database Query.

* Click the System DSN tab.

* Select the DSN (Data Source Name) which connects to your
  BI Connector for Atlas.

* Click OK.

Enter the username and password used to
connect to BI Connector for Atlas and click Ok.

> **Note:** When specifying a username, include the authentication
> database for the user. For example, `salesadmin?source=admin`.

- In the left side of the dialog box, click your server name to expand
  the list collections in your database.

- Select the collection from the list from which contains the data
  you want to import.

- To view your data before importing, click Run to run
  the generated SQL statement. Your data appears in the
  table below the statement.

- Click Return Data.

* Select how you would like to import the data into Excel.

  You can choose to import the data into:

  - An Existing Sheet, specifying in which cell to begin
    the table.

  - A New Sheet, automatically beginning the table in
    cell `A1`.

  - A PivotTable in a new sheet.

* Click OK to complete the import process.
